title: Level 887
wikitext:
{{Foreground|File=200w.gif}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:'''''Level 887: "The Isle of Fog"'''''}}__NOTOC__ {{Class 0|safety=Mostly Safe|security=Sustained Communities|entity=Presence of Wildlife|hexagon=𖠰}} {{Blurimage|file=distortedforest.jpg|caption=A colourized photo of the Forests of Level 887}} ==Description== Level 887 is a temperate pine tree forest consisting of cave openings, lakes, and hills. The level is encompassed within a fog, allowing one to see 20 meters, to 3 meters depending where one is. The trees have found to become increasingly dense overtime, being a main cause of small sinkholes. Additionally, trees have shown to grow into mature stage on an average of 16 days, this not being impacted by factor like water absorption, auxin amount, etc. Weather conditions are consistent throughout the 2 seasons; average temperatures ranging from 19c to 22c in summer, 7c to 14c in winter, showers being rare, and wind has not been present. During winter, wanderers have found to be more drowsy and fatigued; notably, the effects being slightly negated if they were born during winter. The level is an undeterminable amount of distance, however confirmed to be a floating island. The 52 hour day cycle changes instantly from day to night. The forest has found to be “eerie”, or “uncanny” as coined by many wanderers. Furthermore, paranoia has been observed in those who stay for a prolonged period. Caves are known to be dangerous to enter, as most have not returned after entering a cave. Recording and reports of what lurks within cave mostly consists of the wanderers seeing things, and then suddenly dropping on the floor in a few minutes. After analysing bodies out of the cave, the wanderers were discovered to be in a coma state, although EEG results have resulted in constant gamma brain rays. However, those who entered the cave long enough to be put unconscious, however not long or a prolonged coma, they wake up in 3 days to 2 weeks later. Watchtowers can be present throughout the level, consisting of a room that has a bed, a sink and a stove, and a fireplace. Most watchtowers are stocked with hard water, marshmallows, and a pocket knife. Additionally, one can find books written in an undocumented variant of the Sanskrit language. Partial translations of these books have shown context varying to encounters in caves, journals, etc. For unknown reasons, the inside of the watchtower is constantly 5 Celsius cooler then the outside. An abandoned wooden cabin can be present near starting points of the level, comprising documentation of the level as well as its landmarks in the format of sticky notes. These sticky notes has existed since when the level was discovered. It is unknown who left it there or who made the notes. Getting rid of the notes will result in it appearing again in 1 hour. Notably, whatever language the wanderer speaks, the notes will be in. Rarely, one can find books that consist of important survival tips for the Backrooms. However, these spawn rarely in the cabin. If one leaves a belonging in the cabin, the item(s) will disappear. ==The Mountains== There are 3 main mounts throughout the center of the level. [[M.E.G.|M.E.G]] has made numerous hiking trails, however most don’t last 2 to 3 months either due to subsidence, corrosion, or avalanches. The mountains also contain cave openings. Unlike the ground area, these caves have no hazards. Additionally, wildlife can be spotted frequently near these caves, especially in the more snowy parts. Otherwise, one can find eagles, mountain hares, goats, reindeer and snow leopards. One must be extra cautious of the wildlife, due to the higher oxygen levels in the level, wildlife have shown to be 30% larger compared to their [[The Frontrooms|frontrooms]] counterpart. == Eyjafjallajökull == {{Blurimage|file=new icelandic mountain.jpg|caption=Only photo taken of Eyjafjallajökull}} Eyjafjallajökull is a volcanic mountain and is the tallest one in the level. Multiple hike trails have been made, and 2 are still available. However; the mountain is found to be steep and unstable, hike trails cutting off at times. The mountain is measured to be 4,783 metres tall from sea level. ==Я== Mountain Я(ya) is the shortest mountain on this level and has 2 hiking trails and is safe for explorations. A research station (Res Exploration Station) and multiple lookout towers have been built on this mountain. This mountain is 580 meters tall, and is the least steep. However, subsidence is the most common here due to water systems below. Due to this, most of the trees and slopes are cleared. ==Kangerlussuaq Uummannaq== {{Audio|file=Heavy_Blizzard_Sound_Effect_(getmp3.pro).mp3}} Kangerlussuaq Uummannaq is the second tallest mountain at 1,960 to 2,000 meters. The mountain has 32 safe hiking trails and it is known for its annual avalanches, which destroyed the Kangerl South Research Statement in 2015. The mountain recently has found to have mild volcanic activity, some vents appearing out on the top side of the mountain. Other than that, small plumes have been gathering around the mountain every 2 years. ==Wildlife== The wildlife in Level 887 is limited; only a majority of the mammals and Canadian insects. As mentioned before, most of the wildlife has a mild size difference due to the higher oxygen levels on this level. Most mammals have found to be 20% bigger than their [[The Frontrooms|frontrooms]] counterparts. The only exception being the Arctic fox, being 50% bigger. It should be noted due to the enclosed and gradually competitive environment, an animal may attack if close to or seen.
==Note that was found in the Cabin:== ---- ==1/17/15== ---- I have been stuck in these caves forever. Right now, I'm lost in the caves of the Isle of Fog. This place is dark, wet, and I'm starting to lose hope of getting out. I should’ve not gone beyond the do not enter signs. But did I listen? Nope, and now I'm regreding it. I hear all sorts of weird noises that echoing around the caves. My flashlight's about to die, and I don't know how long it'll last. I have tried retracing my steps, but every twist and turn looks the exact same. The air is hard to breathe. Im starving and abou... And the note ends right there since the note is ripped.
==Colonies And Outposts== {{Blurimage|file=RedesignKodia.png|caption=Flag of Kodia}} [[File:887map.png|thumb|321x321px|
A map of Level 887
]] ==Kodia== Kodia is found on the western shore and is named after the cod fish. *Kodia has 37 members, making it the largest colony on the level. *The colony contains wooden cabins. *Kodia is divided into Northern Kodia (12 members) and Southern Kodia (25 members), separated by a river. *Tall log palisades surround the colony's territory. *Primary food sources include cod, beef, and fruit/vegetables. *Kodia is partnered with [[M.E.G.]] and engages in trade with them. ==Colony of Swångdi== *It is located further east and primarily speaks Swedish. *Located near lake Ny Vättern (New Vättern), which the colony has claimed as its own. *Approximately 12 members. *Information about the colony is limited as it is largely private and self-sustaining. ==Казсетгуив (Kazsetguiv) Colony== *Small colony located around a mile east of Kodville. *Situated in a ravine around a river flowing through Я. *Primarily Russian-speaking. *Consits of 18 members. ==Entrances & Exits== ==Entrances== *Finding the asphalt road during event 3 on [[Level 186]] and then walking along it will lead here. *Noclipping through a pine tree on [[Level 45]] will lead here. *Noclipping through a green door on [[Level 410]] will lead here. *Going through a door that has a tree or says level 887 on [[Level 877]] will lead here. ==Exits== *Noclipping through a odd looking tree will lead to [[Level 455]] *Some say entering a icy cave in the mountains will lead to [[Level 74]] but it is unconfirmed. ---- {{Collapse|num=4|initial=
